twitter_thread: 73% of home offices waste $100/yr on inkjet printers when a $50 laser printer would do
Replaced my inkjet with a Brother HL-L2300D laser printer, saved $120 in 6 months
Ditch the $1000 standing desk, a $20 ergonomic keyboard tray fixes posture just as well
Home office mistakes: using a $500 desk lamp when a $15 LED strip does the same job
Spent $30 on noise-cancelling headphones, increased focus by 30% in 3 weeks
Canceled subscription to 'smart' paper tray, saved $60/yr on useless features
What's the cheapest home office upgrade that's had the biggest impact on your productivity? #homeoffice #frugal
pack.linkedin: By switching to a $50 Brother HL-L2300D laser printer, I saved $120 in 6 months. Similarly, a $20 ergonomic keyboard tray can fix posture issues. Other common mistakes to avoid include overpriced desk lamps and subscription-based 'smart' gear. What's the most cost-effective upgrade you've made to your home office, and what were the results?
